projects.name: Project Alpha Demo
projects.description: Example governance and delivery risk tracking project for demos and training.

likelihood.label: Rare
likelihood.description: Unlikely to occur except in exceptional circumstances.

likelihood.label: Unlikely
likelihood.description: Could occur, but not expected during normal execution.

likelihood.label: Possible
likelihood.description: Reasonably plausible and should be considered in planning.

likelihood.label: Likely
likelihood.description: Expected to occur in many realistic scenarios.

likelihood.label: Almost Certain
likelihood.description: Expected to occur frequently or is already emerging.

impact.label: Minimal
impact.description: Minor effect with little operational disruption.

impact.label: Minor
impact.description: Limited impact requiring small local adjustments.

impact.label: Moderate
impact.description: Noticeable impact to delivery, cost, or performance.

impact.label: Major
impact.description: Serious disruption requiring management attention and recovery effort.

impact.label: Severe
impact.description: Critical impact to mission, compliance, or core service continuity.

risks.title: Cloud Latency Spike
risks.statement: IF regional traffic surges exceed current throttling thresholds, THEN platform response times degrade for priority workflows, RESULTING IN missed service commitments and customer churn risk.
risks.trigger: regional traffic surges exceed current throttling thresholds
risks.consequence: platform response times degrade for priority workflows
risks.resultingIn: missed service commitments and customer churn risk
risks.mitigation: Scale multi-region redundancy and tighten dynamic traffic shaping during peak windows.
risks.contingency: Fail over read-heavy traffic to the alternate region if latency remains above threshold for more than 5 minutes.

history.label:
Likelihood updated (3 Possible → 4 Likely)
Rationale: Regional traffic tests showed demand spikes now exceed the old planning assumption during two consecutive release windows.

Impact updated (4 Major → 5 Severe)
Rationale: The commercial cutover increased the number of customer-facing workflows affected by a latency event.

risks.title: Data Residency Compliance Shift
risks.statement: IF EU residency obligations tighten after the policy update, THEN current data handling controls may become non-compliant, RESULTING IN remediation cost and release delays.
risks.trigger: EU residency obligations tighten after the policy update
risks.consequence: current data handling controls may become non-compliant
risks.mitigation: Map hosted datasets to residency zones and complete counsel review before architecture sign-off.
risks.contingency: Pause rollout in affected regions and apply temporary data segregation rules.

history.label:
Impact updated (5 Severe → 4 Major)
Rationale: Counsel confirmed the policy revision narrowed the affected dataset scope, reducing the projected remediation burden.

Likelihood updated (4 Likely → 3 Possible)
Rationale: The draft regulation slipped one review cycle, so the trigger is still credible but less imminent than originally expected.

risks.title: Database Replication Lag
risks.statement: IF replication lag increases during overnight sync windows, THEN downstream reporting may use stale records, RESULTING IN operational rework and decision delay.
risks.trigger: replication lag increases during overnight sync windows
risks.consequence: downstream reporting may use stale records
risks.mitigation: Rebalance jobs and reduce write contention during reconciliation windows.
risks.contingency: Fallback to delayed morning distribution if replication exceeds threshold.

history.label:
Likelihood updated (4 Likely → 3 Possible)
Rationale: Overnight job staggering reduced the frequency of replication lag alerts across the last two reporting cycles.

Impact updated (4 Major → 3 Moderate)
Rationale: Business teams now have a validated morning fallback report, which lowers operational disruption if lag recurs.

risks.title: Vendor Patch Delay
risks.statement: IF the authentication vendor delays the scheduled patch, THEN unresolved vulnerabilities remain in production longer, RESULTING IN elevated exposure and exception requests.
risks.trigger: the authentication vendor delays the scheduled patch
risks.consequence: unresolved vulnerabilities remain in production longer
risks.mitigation: Apply compensating monitoring and track vendor patch verification daily.
risks.contingency: Escalate contractual remediation and isolate exposed workflows.

history.label:
Impact updated (5 Severe → 4 Major)
Rationale: The vulnerable workflow was segmented behind an additional approval gate, lowering the worst-case business impact while the patch is pending.

Likelihood updated (5 Almost Certain → 4 Likely)
Rationale: The vendor provided a verified interim control and a firm patch date, which reduced but did not eliminate the chance of exposure.

decisions.title: Zero-Trust Protocol V2
decisions.summary: Require per-session verification across internal services and retire long-lived tokens.
decisions.context: Long-lived session tokens have been identified as a lateral movement risk in two active risk records. Current authentication architecture relies on tokens valid for up to 24 hours, which extends the blast radius of any credential compromise. An upcoming SOC 2 audit requires demonstrable controls around session management.
decisionDrivers: Reduce lateral movement risk from compromised credentials
Align with SOC 2 Type II session control requirements
Limit blast radius of any single identity compromise
consideredOptions: Adopt per-session verification (Zero-Trust V2) across all internal services
Keep current token duration with added anomaly detection
Pilot zero-trust only on privileged services, defer broad rollout
decisions.outcome: We adopted per-session verification across all internal services and retired long-lived tokens. The pilot-only option was rejected because partial coverage would leave high-value internal APIs unprotected during the audit window. Anomaly detection alone was judged insufficient as a primary control.
goodConsequences: Significantly reduces lateral movement risk if credentials are compromised
Satisfies SOC 2 session management controls ahead of audit
Establishes a consistent identity posture across all internal service boundaries
badConsequences: Increases authentication overhead per request — latency impact must be monitored
Requires coordinated rollout across multiple platform teams
decisions.moreInfo: Implementation tracked under RSK-0024 and RSK-0018. Architecture review completed Apr 07, 2026. Rollout plan owned by Platform team.

decisions.title: Regional Data Residency Migration
decisions.summary: Move regulated datasets into region-specific storage and processing boundaries.
decisions.context: Pending EU policy changes may tighten data residency obligations for regulated datasets currently stored in a multi-region bucket. Legal counsel flagged that the current architecture creates ambiguity about which jurisdiction governs specific datasets, creating remediation risk if the policy update is enacted before the next release.
decisionDrivers: Eliminate legal ambiguity over dataset jurisdiction before policy effective date
Avoid release delays due to mid-cycle compliance remediation
Reduce per-exception handling burden on legal and operations teams
consideredOptions: Migrate regulated datasets to region-specific storage boundaries
Apply manual data handling exceptions per dataset on request
Implement a temporary legal hold process and defer migration
decisions.outcome: We decided to migrate all regulated datasets into region-specific storage and processing boundaries before the policy effective date. Manual exceptions were rejected due to operational overhead and error risk at scale. The legal hold process was judged as a short-term workaround that would not survive a compliance audit.
goodConsequences: Removes compliance ambiguity and simplifies legal review for future releases
Reduces operational burden from per-dataset exception handling
badConsequences: Requires engineering effort to re-architect data pipelines per region
Short-term release velocity impact while migration is in progress
decisions.moreInfo: Migration verified complete Apr 02, 2026. Linked to RSK-0023 for ongoing monitoring.

decisions.title: Authentication Vendor Patch Exception
decisions.summary: Accept short-term compensating controls while vendor remediation is in progress.
decisions.context: The authentication vendor has delayed a critical patch by at least 3 weeks. Unresolved vulnerabilities remain in the production identity platform. Replacing the vendor or disabling the service would cause significant disruption. The committee must decide whether to accept the delay with compensating controls or escalate to a wider platform change.
decisionDrivers: Minimise service disruption during vendor remediation window
Maintain acceptable risk exposure through compensating monitoring
Preserve optionality for a vendor replacement if the delay extends
consideredOptions: Accept the delay with enhanced compensating monitoring and daily patch tracking
Temporarily disable the exposed authentication service
decisions.outcome: The committee deferred a wider platform change and accepted the delay with compensating controls. Immediate vendor replacement was rejected due to the complexity and lead time involved. Disabling the service was not feasible without an alternative in place.
goodConsequences: Avoids disruptive vendor change during an active delivery cycle
Daily tracking ensures the delay does not silently extend beyond the agreed window
badConsequences: Compensating controls are not equivalent to the patch — residual risk remains elevated
If the vendor delays beyond the agreed date, escalation options narrow
decisions.moreInfo: Exception expires when vendor confirms patch deployment. Escalation criteria: delay extends past Apr 30, 2026.
